muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server --use-datastar
muxt check

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /events sse(Stream(ctx, send, sendClock))" -}}<main id="main">{{- .Result -}}</main>{{- end -}}
{{- define "Clock" -}}<time id="clock">{{- .Result -}}</time>{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.25
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

// Stream patches two different elements over one event stream: the base send
// callback renders the route template; the sendClock callback renders the
// "Clock" template (the send-prefix is trimmed). Each is its own
// patch-elements frame. Under --use-datastar the route auto-wraps in datastar(...).
func (Server) Stream(ctx context.Context, send func(string) error, sendClock func(string) error) {
	_ = send("hello")
	_ = sendClock("12:00")
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestStream(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/events", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
		t.Fatalf("status = %d, want %d", rec.Code, http.StatusOK)
	}
	got := rec.Body.String()
	for _, want := range []string{
		"data: elements <main id=\"main\">hello</main>\n",
		"data: elements <time id=\"clock\">12:00</time>\n",
	} {
		if !strings.Contains(got, want) {
			t.Fatalf("body = %q, want it to contain %q", got, want)
		}
	}
}
